title: Rotate encrypted persisted fields safely
prompt: Repair the encrypted-field rotation workflow. Each persisted secret has ciphertext plus a key-version value. Rotation must decrypt legacy rows with the key named by their stored version, re-encrypt them with the active key, and atomically persist the new ciphertext with the active version. Data written with either the old or new key must remain decryptable. An authentication failure must stop the run without changing that row or advancing its checkpoint. A retry must safely resume after the last checkpoint, including when a row replacement completed immediately before checkpoint persistence failed. Logs may identify records and key versions but must never contain plaintext. Keep the public contracts intact and do not modify the protected tests. Verification is offline and succeeds with `python3 -B tests/verify.py` from the repository root.
